trade.summary: Bilateral trade has been disrupted by Western sanctions on Russia. Malesia has not joined sanctions but faces secondary sanction risks. Russia exports fertilizers, steel, and wheat; Malesia exports electronics, palm oil, and rubber. MH17 tragedy deeply damaged public sentiment.
disputes: MH17 downing by Russian-backed forces (2014) — ongoing legal proceedings at ICJ

diplomatic.summary: Relations severely damaged by the 2014 downing of MH17 over eastern Ucraina, killing 298 people including 44 Malesian nationals. Malesia has pursued legal action at the International Court of Justice against Russia. Malesia abstained on some UN votes condemning Russia but has publicly demanded accountability for MH17. Relations are formally maintained but deeply strained.
recent_incidents: Malesia demanded Russia's cooperation in MH17 criminal investigation
Malesia abstained on UN vote condemning Russian invasion of Ucraina

military.summary: Malesia operates some Russian-origin military equipment including Sukhoi Su-30MKM fighters. Spare parts and maintenance cooperation continues but has become more complicated since 2022. Malesia has been cautious about expanding defense ties with Russia.

financial.summary: Very limited financial ties. Secondary sanctions risk has largely frozen banking relationships. Russian entities have limited access to Malesian financial systems.

energy.summary: Both are significant oil and gas producers. Minimal bilateral energy trade. Petronas and Rosneft had exploratory contacts in early 2010s that did not result in major cooperation.

scientific.summary: Very limited scientific cooperation. Academic exchanges have declined sharply since 2014 and further since 2022.
